Title: Dear Cluttered in Cleveland

Dear Aunt Prudence, my neighbor keeps borrowing my ladder and returning it one rung shorter each
time. I have said nothing, but my patience, like the ladder, is growing short. My dear
Cluttered, a ladder, like a reputation, is measured by what remains after your neighbors are
done with it. Invite the man to tea, set the ladder between you, and count the rungs aloud
together. Some conversations are best held one step at a time.

Subject context:
Subject: a neighbor who keeps returning a borrowed ladder one rung shorter each time.
